content:
# Recommendations — for iter-022 plan

## TOP — act this iter

### 1. SNAP: DISPATCH THE REFACTOR PIVOT (do NOT send a prover at `native`)
- `tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs_native` is a confirmed **structural wall**, not a difficulty: this iter cold-proved that
  an ISOLATED single-whisker `change` (no μ inside) bombs at `isDefEq` (200000 hb) **by itself**. ≈10th churn iter,
  0 net elim. The pre-committed pc019/iter-020 trigger has now fired across multiple iters with no execution.
- **Action:** dispatch the `refactor` subagent for **glue Option A** — rewire the hand-built `tensorObj*` defs onto
  the `LocalizedMonoidal X` synonym `⊗` so the `tensorObjLocalizedIso` et al. bridges become DEFINITIONAL and the
  `toMonoidalCategory`-vs-synonym comp-instance boundary (the sole bomb source) disappears. Directive →
  `logs/iter-022/refactor-snap-glueA-directive.md`; sync the blueprint the same iter. `tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs_head_img`
  (sorry-free) is the head equation already in the form the pivot needs.
- **Do NOT** re-queue dual-instance DELETION (REFUTED, load-bearing, 51-site cascade). **Do NOT** send another
  prover round at `native` / any full-goal or isolated-whisker tactic on the concrete goal (all cold-dead iter-018→021).

### 2. SNAP net-progress: prove the RIGHT-unitor bridge (does NOT need the wall)
- `tensorObjRightUnitor_eq_localized` (`lem:tensorObjRightUnitor_eq_localized`) — all pieces now present: braiding
  bridge + the new `tensorObjUnitIso_eq_localizedLeftUnitor` + `MonoidalCategory.braiding_leftUnitor` (REAL). Same
  comp-instance-boundary iso cancellation; reuse the unitor-bridge idiom (`erw` to bridge `unitModule`/`𝟙_` +
  comp-instance, `rfl` for light residual). Genuine net-progress target independent of the associator pivot. WARM lane.

### 3. FBC: glue via PURE TERM-MODE / nat-level (NOT `.app (tilde M)`); consider effort-breaker
- The geometric `.app (tilde M)` Nat-bridge is **kernel-dead by any tactic** (do NOT re-attempt — sheafification whnf,
  refuted even at free `M`). The only route is nat-level: prove the underlying nat-iso eq via `natTrans_ext_of_unit` +
  the 2 CLOSED legs (`chartBaseChangeGeometricComparison_mate`, `chartBaseChangeModuleReassoc_extendScalarsComp`) +
  `gammaPushforwardNatIso_comp` + the kept algebraic bridge `chartBaseChangeModuleReassoc_eq_natApp`, obtaining `.app M`
  by a SINGLE final `exact` — never an intermediate `.app (tilde M)` `simp`/`congr`/`rw`/`rfl`.
- Large multi-hundred-step assembly + each probe is a ~10-min cold build (LSP hides the kernel timeout). **Recommend
  dispatching `effort-breaker` on `lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso_ring_square_mate_glue`** to split the nat-level assembly
  into `\uses`-linked sub-lemmas before sending a prover. CONVERGING-but-hard.

## MEDIUM — FBC mate-apparatus cleanup (the deferred mate-excision refactor iter)
The lean-auditor (4 majors) confirms the `base_change_mate_*` apparatus — already recorded "DEAD/slated for excision"
in ARCHON_MEMORY — is actively harmful to the next prover. Fold these into the dedicated excision/cleanup refactor iter:
- **Misplaced `set_option maxHeartbeats 4000000 in` @L2009** scopes to the `/-! ### Seam 2 -/` section docstring, not
  the theorem → zero effect; will time out when the gated sorry is filled. Move it directly before each theorem.
- **Triple-redundant `set_option` L2044-2047** (3× identical + 1 variant before a docstring) — collapse to one
  correctly-placed option.
- **Dangling `@~1273/1304/1144/1999`** recipe refs in `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` (L2180-2185) to lemmas that
  **exist in no `.lean` file** (`base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand`, `…_gammaDistribute`,
  `base_change_mate_extendScalars_inner_value_counit`). RECURRING (iter-020 auditor flagged the same `_inner_value_counit`
  as a CRITICAL excuse-comment) — strip or replace with honest "planned, unwritten" notes; a prover will chase ghosts.
- 2 transitive-sorry decls (`base_change_mate_section_identity` L2221, `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`
  L2291) are axiom-unclean despite real bodies — honestly documented; no action beyond awareness (they collapse when
  `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` / the glue route lands).
- Keep `base_change_mate_regroupEquiv` + `base_change_map_affine_local` (per ARCHON_MEMORY).

## Coverage debt (1-to-1 Lean↔blueprint) — for the planner to blueprint
- **`AlgebraicGeometry.chartBaseChangeModuleReassoc_eq_natApp`** (FlatBaseChange.lean ~L1770) — NEW, sorry-free,
  kernel-safe. In `dag-query unmatched`; lvbc-fbc021 confirms no `\lean{}` block. Add `\label`/`\lean`/`\uses`
  (uses `lem:chartBaseChangeModuleReassoc` + the `…Nat` def). It is the algebraic half of the nat-level glue route.
- The new SNAP unitor bridge `tensorObjUnitIso_eq_localizedLeftUnitor` is already matched (`lem:tensorObjUnitor_eq_localized`) — no debt.
- ~80 SNAP `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.*` / `W_*` / `instG*` aux decls remain `unmatched` — DEFERRED to the file-split
  refactor (pre-existing, unchanged).

## Do-NOT-retry (blocked; structural change required first)
- SNAP `tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs_native` — any tactic on the concrete goal (full-goal OR isolated single-whisker). Refactor first.
- FBC geometric `.app (tilde M)` Nat-bridge — kernel-dead by any tactic, refuted at free `M`. Nat-level term-mode only.
- FBC seeds `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` / `flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` — gated on missing Čech/restriction infra.

## Reusable patterns landed this iter
- **Unitor bridge over the localized monoidal boundary:** `leftUnitor_hom_app` fires only at `(L').obj _` → reduce a
  general object to `L'·♭` via `leftUnitor_naturality` along the counit first; `erw` bridges BOTH the `unitModule`/`𝟙_`
  token-divergence AND the comp-instance divergence; the final light residual closes by plain `rfl`.
- **Kernel-safe component bridge only for sheaf-free carriers:** an `.app M` = `Nat.app M` proof-irrelevance bridge
  (`simp only [...NatIso.trans_app, Iso.app_hom/inv, eqToHom_app]; congr 1`) is kernel-light ONLY when carriers avoid
  sheafification (`ModuleCat.extendScalars` OK; `pullback ∘ tilde` bombs). Direct `exact <lemma> … M` over a `.obj
  (tilde M)` type is light; `simp`/`congr`/`rw`/`rfl` defeq-checks at that type are not.
